Birds of a Feather - Attendee organized sessions
items.location: Location Salon B - (Birds of a Feather Rooms)
items.description: Birds of a Feather sessions, or BoFs, are un-conference style sessions where community members self-organize to have a round table discussion about a particular topic.

items.title: Summit: AI (guests must pre-register)
items.description: NOTE: Summits require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this summit during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this summit, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Summit: Community (guests must pre-register)
items.description: The Drupal Community Summit at DrupalCon is a collaborative, discussion-focused gathering where people from across the Drupal ecosystem — from newcomers to long-time contributors — come together to connect, share experiences, and explore ways to get involved with and strengthen the community.

items.title: Summit: Government (guests must pre-register)
items.description: NOTE: Summits require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this summit during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this summit, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Summit: Healthcare (guests must pre-register)
items.description: NOTE: Summits require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this summit during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this summit, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Summit: Higher Education (guests must pre-register)
items.description: NOTE: Summits require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this summit during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this summit, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Summit: Non-Profit (guests must pre-register)
items.description: NOTE: Summits require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this summit during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this summit, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Training: Future Proof Theming: Best Practices for Drupal’s New Era
items.description: NOTE: Trainings require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this training during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this training, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Summit: Drupal Partner (guests must pre-register)
items.description: NOTE: Summits require an add-on ticket. Guests can pre-register for this summit during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add this summit, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Mentor Orientation
Welcome Reception
General Contribution / New Member + Local Drupal Community Meeting Space
Quiet Room
REGISTRATION
Birds of a Feather - Attendee organized sessions
items.location: Location Salon B - (Birds of a Feather Rooms)
items.description: Birds of a Feather sessions, or BoFs, are un-conference style sessions where community members self-organize to have a round table discussion about a particular topic.

items.title: Coffee & New Connections (invitation only)
items.description: Putting the "unity" in community, this invite-only group welcomes participants attending DrupalCon Chicago through Community and Inclusion invitations. Please join us for donuts, coffee, and a chance to fuel your Tuesday morning.

items.title: EXPO HALL COFFEE BREAK
A Dashboard that Works: Giving Editors What They Want, But Focusing on What They Need
items.description: When 500 editors across 130 sites log into our Drupal platform, the first thing they see is a dashboard designed to support their work. But what should that dashboard actually do? Over the last year, we’ve been figuring out how to answer that question.

items.title: Config Pages: The Missing Admin UI for Your Custom Site Settings
items.description: Most custom features in Drupal need settings, but too often, those settings are hidden behind broad admin permissions, hardcoded into modules or themes, or require custom forms just to expose them.

items.title: Drupal CMS, site templates and beyond
items.description: Drupal CMS 2.0 has launched, complete with Drupal Canvas and site templates! What does this mean for building Drupal sites? And what's next?

items.title: Drupal Test Traits - Learn by example
items.description: Learn about Drupal Test Traits (DTT), an open source project started by the Commonwealth of Massachusetts. Dive into real tests from our open source project, and learn how DTT works under the hood.

items.title: First-Time Contributor Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Chicago to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.title: From Insights to Innovation: How UX Research is driving the future of Drupal CMS
items.description: What if Drupal's biggest innovation opportunity comes from studying users, not systems? As Drupal CMS UX Research Lead, my Drupal journey is unconventional. As a content writer and UX professional I am obsessed with watching people use interfaces to spark transformative ideas.

items.title: How to Land an EPIC Contribution in Drupal (Without Losing Your Mind)
items.description: Behind every great Drupal feature is a stubborn person (not necessarily a developer) who refused to let it stay broken. Want to be that person? We’ll show you how.

items.title: Increasing enrolment amidst changing student demographics.
items.description: Higher education is facing a critical challenge: shifting demographics, increasing competition, and evolving student expectations are making enrolment growth harder than ever.

items.title: Innovation at Odds: How Conflict Fuels Great Ideas
items.description: True innovation requires the grappling of ideas—where differing viewpoints, experiences, and expertise collide to create something greater than the sum of their parts.

items.title: The Bug Stops Here: The State of Georgia Shifts Left
items.description: How the State of Georgia streamlined their QA and UAT process to ship code faster and catch bugs earlier.

items.title: KEYNOTE: Welcome & Aaron Winborn Award
KEYNOTE: Driesnote
Black in Drupal Luncheon (guests must pre-register)
items.description: Guests can pre-register for the luncheon during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add the luncheon, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: Lunch & Expo Hall
10 - Minute Lightening Talk: Beyond Card Grids: Using AI to Make Digital Experiences Human Again
items.description: "Come for the code and stay for the community" — that's a phrase that runs deep in the Drupal psyche. It's what pulled me in over 20yrs ago. It might be the same for you. But why is that? It's because yes, tools and the things that we can build with them are fun, but we need more than that.

items.title: 10 - Minute Lightening Talk: Drupal Commerce's Updated Order Management Interface
items.description: Centarro recently released Commerce Core 3.3.0, which includes a brand now order management interface that the whole team participated in building through the second half of 2025. We're excited to show it off here at DrupalCon Chicago!

items.title: Agent Experience (AX) and AI-driven Drupal
items.description: We spend significant time optimizing User and Developer Experience (UX/DX), but our next frontier is Agent Experience (AX).

items.title: Designing for Difference: Practical Strategies for Building a Neuroinclusive Organization
items.description: If recognizing the value of neurodiversity is the first step, the next is changing systems, environments, and behaviours to actually support neurodivergent people. Neuroinclusive spaces use the ethos of Universal Design.

items.title: Mentor Orientation
Misusing...er...uh... Re-purposing Recipes to achieve development bliss
items.description: I want... - Drupal CMS - Configuration management - Automatic updates - Git workflows - Modern infrastructure - Continuous delivery - Project Browser for content editors In the words of Queen, "I want it all... and I want it now!"

items.title: Open Y: A Drupal-Powered Platform for Good
items.description: The YMCA’s mission is rooted in community impact—and in today’s world, that includes a strong, unified digital presence. Enter Open Y, an open-source, Drupal-based platform built specifically for YMCAs to streamline content management, marketing, and integrations.

items.title: Security Team Panel
items.description: The security team will present topics from their work promoting a more secure Drupal project. The team will also talk about how they recruit new members, refinements to their procedures, and what you can do to support Drupal security.

items.title: Separated by an Ocean, United by Drupal: LocalGov & ProvusGov
items.description: Government agencies on both sides of the Atlantic are embracing Drupal to meet the demand for cost-effective, standards-compliant digital services.

items.title: Streamlining Content Relevance, Translation, and Migration with a Content Audit tool
items.description: In this session, we will showcase a custom module designed to assist editors with conducting a content audit, including a ROT analysis (Redundant, Outdated, and Trivial) and translation reviews on their website.

items.title: The Strategic Evolution of Drupal and the AI Initiative - Orchestration and Beyond
items.description: At Vienna, we showcased how Canvas AI can transform page-building in Drupal. Now, we’re excited to show you the next step.

items.title: EXPO HALL COFFEE BREAK
10 - Minute Lightening Talk: The Cost of Waiting: What Happens if You Ignore AI
items.description: AI is moving fast, and those who wait risk to adopt falling behind. But Drupal AI is already enabling developers and businesses to eliminate bottlenecks and inefficiencies and deliver more value through enhanced content, AI search, and smarter site operations.

items.title: 10 - Minute Lightening Talk: CKEditor in Drupal: From Better Formatting to AI-Powered Authoring
items.description: Since our last news session in Atlanta, the editorial landscape has shifted, but CKEditor has continued to deliver continuous value across both open-source and premium modules.

items.title: Context is Everything: Configuring Drupal AI for Consistent, On-Brand Content
items.description: AI can generate landing pages, service pages, and blog posts in seconds - but without the right context, the results feel generic, off-brand, or just plain wrong. Context is what takes you from AI-slop to content that is authentic and resonates with your audience.

items.title: Drupal Association Public Board Meeting
Exposing Your Drupal Site’s Unique Content and Features to LLMs with MCP
items.description: Large Language Models like ChatGPT and Claude are increasingly used for search, support, dev tools, and content creation. Out of the box, they can read but not interact with your Drupal sites content and features.

items.title: Facilitating Like a Dungeon Master: Using RPG Skills for Stakeholder Magic
items.description: For so many of our projects, the key to success lies in mastering the art of listening and interpreting feedback—skills that any seasoned game master knows well.

items.title: From Chaos to Clarity: Building a Sustainable Content Governance Model with Drupal
items.description: Organizations often struggle with fragmented content, inconsistent messaging, and editorial bottlenecks—especially as websites grow and multiple teams contribute to the same platform.

items.title: Future-Proofing Accessibility: Strategies for Government & University Platforms
items.description: Kat Shaw and M. Nikki Flores are CPACC-certified and work with Javier Reartes at Lullabot to integrate accessibility as a mindset and framework into government platforms, such as for the states of Iowa, Maryland, and Georgia, and higher education, such as UMass Amherst and NYU.

items.title: Next Generation ECA - Vision and Progress update
items.description: ECA (the Event, Condition, Action module) for modern Drupal has established itself as the go-to tool for automating Drupal workflows with a no-code/low-code approach.

items.title: Next steps for Drupal Canvas
items.description: For years, the Drupal community has talked about a component-driven future. The Drupal Canvas is where that future finally becomes a reality. This is more than just an evolution of Layout Builder; it's a fundamental paradigm shift in how we think about building with Drupal.

items.title: No Code Queries: Dynamic Content with Views
items.description: Drupal’s Views module is a powerful tool for building custom content listings, filtered displays, and dynamic, auto-updating pages — all without writing any code.

items.title: Stop building AI features
items.description: Every CMS and web tool is racing to add AI content generation, AI image editing, AI assistants. It makes sense — and demos look great! But this approach has a ceiling. We will never out-build the frontier model providers, and the features we ship today will feel outdated in six months.

items.title: Stop creating Drupal Forms! Just describe a schema - the form will appear automatically!
items.description: Oh, that Drupal Forms... Too much code just to create a simple form with a couple of fields! What if I tell you that those times are in the past?

items.title: Expo Hall
Building AI-Powered Drupal Applications: From Setup to Production-Ready Features
items.description: Transform your Drupal sites with practical AI integration in this hands-on workshop.

items.title: Design Logic Unearthed: Code rock-solid sites via the lost art of design interrogation
items.description: It’s a scenario we’ve all lived through: your deadline is fast approaching. UI bugs are cropping up left, right and center. Spacing is so inconsistent it looks jarring, and your client is stressed that every image they upload in Drupal comes out with people’s faces cropped in half.

items.title: Elevating Drupal Experiences with Vanilla Javascript
items.description: Modern web experiences don’t always require decoupled architectures, complex frameworks, or external libraries like React.

items.title: Escape from Drupal Island! (Spice level: 🌶️ 🌶️ 🌶️ )
items.description: It's been 13 years since the original call to "get off the island" went out. Since then the web has grown and changed quite a lot, yet DrupalCon remains a sea of familiar faces.

items.title: Launching the Drupal Site Template Marketplace
items.description: The potential of Site Templates is clear: by combining recipes, demo content, and a Canvas-compatible theme, they make Drupal’s power, flexibility, and openness more accessible—giving users a head start with DrupalCMS.

items.title: Managing Configuration for Drupal Multisites: Config Splits, Templates, and Automation
items.description: Running multiple sites from a single Drupal codebase is powerful—and tricky—when it comes to configuration. One site needs a feature, another must not have that feature; environment overrides creep in; and “just export and import” stops being simple.

items.title: Proposing a 'Code of Care' for the Drupal Community
items.description: Following on from the DrupalCon Vienna session "It’s OK to NOT be OK! Establishing support frameworks in community teams", this session will advance the proposal to create a 'Code of Care' to enshrine in policy how we care for each other in the Drupal community.

items.title: Teaching an old site new tricks: Refreshing the Flagship at Harvard Medical School
items.description: Our talk will present the most salient aspects of what we call the HMS Flagship Refresh project, born out of multiple needs: integrating the MD Program’s content into the site, before OpenScholar’s decommissioning in November 2025; enhancing the role of the site as an editorial product, after the

items.title: The state of JavaScript Code Components in Drupal Canvas
items.description: Drupal Canvas introduced Code Components last year at DrupalCon Atlanta, opening a new avenue for Drupal frontend engineering by shipping a zero-setup, in-browser code editor, and out-of-the-box support for React and Tailwind CSS. A lot has happened since then.

items.title: Winning the Higher Ed CMS Battle: How to Outsell Proprietary Platforms with Drupal
items.description: We know that Drupal is a predominant Content Management System amongst the top 100 universities globally. What about the other 5,000+ institutions? Who’s choosing proprietary CMS’s, when, and why?

items.title: General Contribution / New Member + Local Drupal Community Meeting Space
Quiet Room
REGISTRATION
10 - Minute Lightening Talk: Translate your site more easily with MCP
items.description: Shipping your website in multiple languages means more people can read your content or buy your products. Machine translation makes this easier than ever, but you’ll still want to let humans make the final edits . But what if your humans don’t want to use Drupal?

items.title: Birds of a Feather - Attendee organized sessions
items.location: Location Salon B - (Birds of a Feather Rooms)
items.description: Birds of a Feather sessions, or BoFs, are un-conference style sessions where community members self-organize to have a round table discussion about a particular topic.

items.title: EXPO HALL COFFEE BREAK
Ripple Makers Networking Breakfast (members only) (guest must pre-register)
10 - Minute Lightening Talk: From Ranking to Citation: Optimizing Drupal for the AI Search Era
items.description: Search has evolved from a list of links to a conversation. AI systems like Google’s AI Overviews, ChatGPT and Gemini don’t just “rank” your site – they ingest, interpret and cite it. In this new landscape, visibility depends on semantic clarity and machine-readability.

items.title: A Taste of the Future: Site Templates and Recipes in Drupal
items.description: Drupal's Recipes Initiative is redefining how we build and share functionality, moving beyond distributions toward lightweight, composable recipes and site templates.

items.title: Advanced Site Building with Drupal Canvas
items.description: Enabling ambitious site builders has always been a superpower of Drupal and the introduction of Drupal Canvas has taken this to another level. This session will explore advanced site-building techniques utilizing the new Drupal Canvas module.

items.title: Beyond Chatbots: Creating Smarter, Personalized Experiences in Drupal with AG-UI
items.description: We are witnessing a fundamental shift in how businesses utilize Artificial Intelligence. The era of simple, text-based chatbots is ending.

items.title: Breaking the Theme Barrier: Simplifying Drupal with SDC & SDC Display
items.description: Drupal's Single Directory Components (SDC) represent a significant shift in how we approach component-based theming by grouping templates, CSS, and JavaScript into a single directory.

items.title: Deploy with Confidence: Automated Testing for Drupal
items.description: Shipping updates for Drupal sites doesn’t have to be stressful or error-prone.

items.title: Display Builder, the HTMX-powered visual builder
items.description: HTMX has been added to Drupal 11.2 as a light javascript dependency to modernize and unify many legacy Drupal APIs: AJAX, AJAX for Views, Form States, Big Pipe…

items.title: Headings the Right Way: Site Building Tips for Accessible Page Content
items.description: For websites to be accessible, content needs to be thoughtfully arranged into sections and navigable by headings, so users can easily scan a page and find the information they are seeking.

items.title: Selling Drupal: How to win projects, and not alienate delivery teams
items.description: All too frequently the relationship between sales and delivery is a constant balancing act. Sales teams are driven by ambitious targets, while delivery teams are focused on accurate scoping, sustainable recovery rates, and the practical realities of open-source implementation. The result?

items.title: Steal This Playbook: UX Lessons From Nonprofit Websites
items.description: Great non-profit websites do more than deliver content, they create connection. They guide users, inspire engagement and donations, and make complex organizations feel human.

items.title: KEYNOTE: Alexandra Bell, The Security Implications of AI
items.description: AI technologies are evolving faster than our ability to understand them, much less control them.

items.title: Lunch & Expo Hall
Women in Drupal Luncheon (guests must pre-register)
items.description: Guests can pre-register for the luncheon during the DrupalCon Chicago registration process. If you’re already registered and would like to add the luncheon, simply use the “Modify” button in your confirmation email to update your registration.

items.title: 10 Ways to Learn AI in Drupal (Live Examples and Ready-to-Use Templates)
items.description: How do you learn to cook? Try the dish first, watch someone make it, deconstruct the recipe, then make it yourself. Same with fixing cars, playing music, or any skill. But learning AI in Drupal is hard - where are the working examples to try?

items.title: Beyond the Drupal bubble: Building a cross-community ecosystem
items.description: Drupal has always thrived because of its passionate developer community. But the future of Drupal depends on something bigger: the ecosystem around it. Designers, communicators, strategists, web managers, and decision-makers all influence whether Drupal gets adopted and sustained.

items.title: Designing for Depth: Information Architecture Strategies in Large Drupal Sites
items.description: When your Drupal site spans thousands of pages, good content structure isn’t just helpful, it’s essential. This 30-minute session will share practical strategies for managing large-scale Drupal sites through user-centered information architecture (IA) and smart use of Drupal’s tools.

items.title: Drupal developer's guide to Canvas
items.description: This technical session covers the high-level architecture and inner workings of Drupal Canvas. The session focuses on the back-end aspects that Drupal developers need to know when developing with future Canvas adoption in mind.

items.title: Engineering for security compliance: How to prepare before the audit
items.description: Security and privacy compliance certifications—like SOC 2 (a leading audit standard for security, availability, and confidentiality) and HITRUST (a healthcare-focused security framework)—are quickly becoming standard requirements for healthcare, finance, and other regulated industries building on

items.title: First-Time Contributor Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Chicago to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.title: Publish once in Drupal, Syndicate Everywhere
items.description: Drupal excels at the Create Once, Publish Everywhere (COPE) model—using structured content to enable the same individual piece of content to flow into a variety of contexts.

items.title: Strategies for Integrating Drupal Canvas in Your Existing Drupal Platform
items.description: Explore practical strategies for adopting Drupal's Drupal Canvas in existing platforms, ensuring a smooth transition without compromising current investments.

items.title: You can (and should!) build a site template
items.description: Site templates are a big deal. They have the potential to open Drupal up to more people than ever by delivering functional and beautiful starter sites. But it's up to the Drupal community to create them. And, as with anything in the Drupal world, there are a few ways to go about it.

items.title: EXPO HALL COFFEE BREAK
A peek behind the curtain on how we run an open source, global, and privacy-first AI Infrastructure
items.description: In this session, we will let you all see behind the curtain of amazee.ai. This will not be a sales pitch in any way, but rather an in depth overview how AI infrastructure is ran today on a global scale.

items.title: AI as a PM’s Co-Pilot: Decision-Making with the OODA Loop
items.description: As a project manager, I remember how overwhelming it was to handle that big Drupal multi-site rollout. There were so many tickets to track, and the client’s priorities kept shifting.

items.title: Building Enterprise AI Tools: From Concept to Production
items.description: Organizations rush to implement AI but see minimal ROI by treating it as a feature rather than a fundamental shift in how teams work. This gap costs companies millions in wasted licenses and missed opportunities.

items.title: From Search Bars to Chatbots: Decoding User Expectations for AI Chat
items.description: Chat bubbles are everywhere, but are they actually working for users? Sandstorm® conducted usability studies with actual users to uncover what people really want when interacting with AI-driven tools.

items.title: Frontend Strategies for Drupal: From Multisite to Migrations
items.description: Drupal’s frontend ecosystem continues to evolve, and the right frontend strategy depends on the project context. Rather than a narrow “how-to” on a specific editor or framework, this session looks across types of Drupal frontend projects and the strategies that make them successful.

items.title: KEYNOTE: Drupal CMS Spotlights
items.description: Dive deeper into the latest developments of Drupal CMS and the AI Initiative in a series of lightning talks. The speakers are not only experts in their field, but they are the ones leading each area of development or research.

items.title: Migrate Smarter: How a Structured AI Methodology Unlocked a Complex Drupal 7 to 11 Migration
items.description: Migrating a 15-year-old Italian humor site (132K nodes, 100K newsletter subscribers, 18 custom modules, 93 contrib modules, and a MailUp integration) to Drupal 11 is the kind of project that traditionally requires a team, months of work, and a budget that's hard to swallow.

items.title: Preparing your Pipeline for the AI Era
items.description: Whether you're an AI optimist, a pessimist, or somewhere in-between, there's no getting around it: if you aren't shipping AI-generated code yet, you will be. Are you and your team prepared for it?

items.title: Serious Results, Surprisingly Fun: A Playbook for KPI-Driven Digital Transformation
items.description: Need a little joy right now? This story has it all: success metrics exceeded, meaningful digital transformation, and a team that genuinely enjoyed the journey. As one usability test participant put it: “I can’t wait until this is live!”

items.title: Using Drupal Doesn't Make You Open Source — Lessons from Europe's Largest Public Sector Platform
items.description: Today the European Commission runs one of the largest Drupal platforms in the world. In the beginning it was adoption without contribution. Each institution tried to implement similar solutions.

items.title: Workspaces is revolutionizing Drupal Core: Unlock True Enterprise Content Management in Drupal
items.description: To be a true enterprise content management system, Drupal must support comprehensive, site-wide content staging.

items.title: Expo Hall
AI Agents for Site Builders
items.description: What if you could open up the world of Agents, so that people with communication and language skills could focus on the prompt writing and the actual way the Agents interact with Drupal or its content is just an abstraction that you get access to with a click of a button.

items.title: AI Crawlers Are Crushing Your Website: Here's What You Can Do About It
items.description: Over the past year, a new kind of bot has shown up in our server logs: AI-powered crawlers scraping public government websites to feed large language models.

items.title: Beyond Chunk and Pray: Smarter RAG for Drupal AI
items.description: Everyone’s talking about RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation), but let’s be honest: the standard demo usually goes something like this: split your content into chunks, toss them into a vector database, cross your fingers, and hope the LLM comes back with something vaguely useful.

items.title: Drupal AI Initiative: A Year in Review with Panel Q&A
items.description: With the launch of the Drupal AI Initiative approaching its one-year anniversary, now is the perfect time for a critical look back and an exciting preview of what's next.

items.title: Drupal.org Engineering Panel
items.description: Join the Drupal.org engineering team for updates on our work to support the community.

items.title: Get Aligned or Stay Stuck: How Experiments Unlock Team Potential
items.description: Team's aren't getting stuck because they lack ideas -- they get stuck because they can’t agree on what to try next. Whether it’s due to navigating internal politics, a fear of failure, or decision fatigue, progress becomes a never-ending churn when a path forward struggles to be defined.

items.title: HTMX: a new toolset in Drupal Core
items.description: HTMX has steadily risen among JavaScript projects since its release in recent years, gaining more new stars on GitHub in 2024 than React. Now HTMX is integrated into Drupal core and forms the basis of a subsystem that will replace our jQuery based Ajax API.

items.title: Let the Browser Decide: Responsive Images as a Tool to Bridge the Digital Divide
items.description: A major contributing factor to the Digital Divide - the unequal access to and effective use of digital technology - lies squarely with those of us who build and maintain websites.

items.title: General Contribution / New Member + Local Drupal Community Meeting Space
Mentored Contribution
items.description: If you are already familiar with the tools the Drupal community uses (such as the issue queue and/or a local development environment), but could still use help choosing issues and navigating the process, Mentored Contribution is for you.

items.title: Quiet Room
REGISTRATION
Drupal in a Day
First-Time Contributor Workshop
items.description: Do you want to contribute but are not sure how? Check out the First Time Contributor Pathway for a list of recommended sessions at DrupalCon Chicago to help you figure out how to use your particular skills to contribute to the Drupal project!

items.title: Trivia Night
items.description: DrupalCon Chicago Trivia Night will be held on Thursday, March 26 from 6pm - 9pm at the Weather Mark Tavern (1503 S Michigan Ave, Chicago, IL 60605).
